findings.description: No candidate rules exist for this blob at all, despite it containing a substantive, closed set of approved gradient specifications plus usage and directional constraints. The entire blob was dropped from extraction.

payload.statement: Only the seven listed approved gradients may be used; do not invent new gradients.

findings.description: The heading explicitly states gradients are an 'approved set — never invent gradients' and the closing list item restates 'Approved values only.' These are strong compliance/governance statements (hard prohibition on inventing new gradients) that must be captured with hardness=hard and polarity=must_not on invention, separate from the allowed-usage rule.

payload.statement: Never invent gradients outside the approved set; use only approved gradient values.

findings.description: Missed relation: the masthead gradient row (u_0008, bolded as 'the gradient to use with the logo mark') and the directional/orientation constraint in u_0009 ('Gradients run horizontally in masthead/banner applications... left → right as shown') describe the same masthead usage context and should be linked or merged into one usage-scoped rule rather than left as independent, unclustered facts.

payload.statement: Use the Sunshine → light gold gradient for masthead/logo-mark contrast, oriented dark/saturated-to-light left-to-right, with the mark at the lightest end.

findings.description: u_0010 bundles three distinct scope facets (backgrounds, wave-graphic combination, logo-mark combination) plus a governance clause ('Approved values only') in one list item; if clustered as a single rule it should be split by scope/usage-context facet rather than treated as one atomic statement, or at minimum each facet should be tagged separately.
